cling.def: 黏著、依附、堅持、緊抓、依靠
cling.ex: The wet shirt clung to his back.

attendance.def: 出席、出席人數、照料、侍候
attendance.ex: Attendance at the meeting is compulsory.

browse.def: 瀏覽、吃草、嫩葉、嫩芽、放牧
browse.col: browse the internet, browse through a book
browse.ex: I spent the afternoon browsing in the library.

clinical.def: 臨床的、診所的、客觀冷靜的
clinical.ex: The drug is currently undergoing clinical trials.

attic.ex: The old books are stored in the attic.

bruise.def: 青腫、傷痕、挫傷、(水果等)撞出的傷痕、使挫傷、使受傷
bruise.ex: She has a bad bruise on her arm.

contend.ex: We have many difficulties to contend with.

decay.def: 腐朽、衰退、放射性衰變、潰落、腐爛、衰減、使枯萎、使衰弱

competent.def: 有能力的、勝任的、足夠的
competent.ex: She is a very competent manager.

continental.ex: The hotel offers a continental breakfast.

deceive.ex: He was deceived into believing that he would get the job.

auxiliary.def: 輔助的、附屬的、預備的、預備軍、助動詞
auxiliary.ex: The ship has an auxiliary engine.

avail.def: 效用、利益、有用、有利、有益於、有助於
avail.col: to little avail, of no avail
avail.ex: Her efforts were to little avail.

available.def: 可利用的、可得到的、有效的、有空的
available.ex: Is technical support available twenty-four hours a day?

avenge.ex: He swore to avenge his father's death.

avenue.ex: We must explore all avenues to find a solution.

average.def: 平均數、一般、平庸、平均的、一般的、平庸的、平均做、平均分配、平均為
average.col: on average, above average, below average
average.ex: The average age of the students is twenty.

averse.ex: He is averse to any kind of change.

aversion.def: 厭惡、反感、令人討厭的事物
aversion.ex: She has a strong aversion to snakes.

avert.col: avert a crisis, avert one's eyes
avert.ex: The government is trying to avert an economic crisis.

aviation.ex: He has spent his life in the field of aviation.

await.ex: We are eagerly awaiting the final results.

awake.def: 醒著的、警覺的、喚醒、使發覺、覺悟、喚起
awake.ex: He was wide awake at three o'clock in the morning.

awaken.ex: The story awakened her interest in science.

award.def: 獎品、裁決、獎學金、授予、判給、給予
award.ex: She was awarded first prize in the competition.

aware.def: 意識到的、知道的、有覺悟的
aware.ex: I was aware of someone watching me.

awareness.ex: The campaign is designed to raise environmental awareness.

awe.def: 敬畏、畏懼、威嚴、使敬畏、使驚嘆
awe.col: in awe of, stand in awe
awe.ex: We stared in awe at the mountain.

awesome.def: 令人敬畏的、極好的、美妙的

awful.def: 極壞的、糟糕的、可怕的、極其、非常
awful.ex: There's an awful smell in the kitchen.

awkward.def: 尷尬的、笨拙的、累贅的、難應付的
awkward.ex: There was an awkward silence in the room.

ax/axe.def: 斧頭、削減、解僱、用斧砍、大舉削減、撤銷
ax/axe.ex: He chopped the wood with an axe.

axis.ex: The earth rotates on its axis.

baby.def: 嬰兒、幼獸、寶貝、幼稚、寵愛的、過份遷就地對待
baby.ex: The baby is crying because he is hungry.

babysit.ex: She babysits for the neighbors once a week.

babysitter.ex: They are looking for a reliable babysitter.

backbone.ex: He is the backbone of the basketball team.

backdrop.ex: The discussion took place against a backdrop of increasing political tension.

background.def: 背景、出身、底色、配角、不顯眼的位置、背景的、配角的
background.ex: She has an excellent educational background.

backing.ex: The project has the financial backing of several large companies.

backlash.ex: The government faces a public backlash over the tax increase.

backpack.def: 背包、裝在背包中、揹背包旅行
backpack.ex: He is planning to backpack around Europe this summer.

backstage.def: 在後台、在幕後、後台的、幕後的
backstage.ex: She was allowed to go backstage to meet the singer.

backward/backwards.def: 向後地、倒退、退步、落後的、向後的、保守的

bacteria.ex: There are billions of bacteria in our body.

bacterial.ex: The doctor prescribed antibiotics for the bacterial infection.

badge.def: 徽章、標記、象徵、授予徽章
badge.ex: She was wearing a badge with her name on it.

baffle.def: 使困惑、阻撓、擋板、緩衝、隔音壁
baffle.ex: The puzzle baffled even the experts.

baggage.ex: What's the baggage allowance for this flight?

bail.def: 保釋金、把手、桶、保釋、舀水、跳傘逃生
bail.ex: The businessman was released on bail.

bait.def: 餌、誘惑、誘餌、裝餌、引誘、嘲弄
bait.ex: He used a worm as bait to fish.

bake.ex: She is baking a chocolate cake for the party.

balance.def: 平衡、天平、餘額、沈著、稱(重量)、平衡、權衡、穩定
balance.col: bank balance, check balance, strike a balance
balance.ex: It's important to keep a balance between work and play.

balcony.ex: He was standing on the balcony watching the sunset.

bald.def: 禿頭的、無毛的、直率的、露出地、單調地

ballad.ex: The singer performed a series of romantic ballads.

ballet.def: 芭蕾舞、芭蕾舞劇、芭蕾舞團
ballet.ex: She has been studying classical ballet since she was five.

balloon.def: 氣球、飛行船、使膨脹、激增、乘氣球飛行、象氣球般漲大
balloon.ex: The children were playing with colorful balloons.

ballot.def: 選票、投票、投票權、投票、抽籤、投票
ballot.ex: The election was held by secret ballot.

ban.def: 禁止、禁令、譴責、禁止、取締、查封
ban.ex: The government has imposed a ban on smoking in public places.

band.def: 樂隊、帶、波段、一群、綁、給...裝上帶子、結合
band.col: rock band, rubber band, marching band
band.ex: He plays the guitar in a rock band.

bandage.ex: The nurse put a bandage on my arm.

bandit.ex: The village was attacked by a group of armed bandits.

bang.def: 砰然一聲、猛擊、劉海、砰地敲、猛撞、激動、砰地一聲
bang.ex: He banged on the door until she opened it.

banish.ex: He was banished from the country for his political views.

bankrupt.def: 破產者、無知的人、破產的、枯竭的、使破產、使枯竭
bankrupt.ex: The company went bankrupt last year.

bankruptcy.ex: The company has filed for bankruptcy.

banner.def: 旗幟、橫幅、標題、大標題、卓越的、一流水準的
banner.ex: The protesters were carrying banners with slogans on them.

banquet.def: 宴會、盛宴、款待、宴會慶祝、參加宴會
banquet.ex: They held a grand banquet to celebrate the occasion.

bar.def: 棒、條、障礙、酒吧、律師協會、排除、禁止、除...之外、除...之外
bar.col: chocolate bar, behind bars (坐牢), bar associations
bar.ex: He was eating a chocolate bar.

barb.def: 倒鉤、刺、倒刺的部分、給...裝上倒鉤
barb.ex: The fence was topped with barbed wire.

barber.ex: I am going to the barber for a haircut.

bare.def: 裸的、僅有的、赤裸、露出、揭開、僅、剛好
bare.col: bare hands, bare feet, lay bare
bare.ex: He walked on the sand with bare feet.

barely.def: 幾乎不、剛好、公開地、赤裸地
barely.ex: We barely had enough time to finish the task.

bargain.def: 便宜貨、交易、協議、討價還價、協議、達成交易
bargain.col: real bargain, strike a bargain, hard bargain
bargain.ex: The shoes were a real bargain at half price.

bark.def: 狗叫、樹皮、船、咆哮、吠、擦傷(皮)
bark.ex: The dog barked at the stranger.

barn.def: 穀倉、馬廄、簡陋的大建築物
barn.ex: The farmer stores the hay in the barn.

barometer.def: 氣壓計、晴雨表、變化標誌
barometer.ex: Stock prices are often seen as an economic barometer.

baron.ex: He is a powerful newspaper baron.

barrack.def: 營房、兵營、起鬨、喝倒彩、住在營房中
barrack.ex: The soldiers returned to their barracks.

barrel.def: 桶、槍管、一大筆、把...裝入桶內、高速行駛
barrel.ex: Beer is stored in wooden barrels.

barren.def: 荒瘠的、不孕的、無趣的、荒地
barren.ex: The land is barren and rocky.

barrier.col: language barrier, trade barrier, break down barriers
barrier.ex: Language can be a major barrier to communication.

barter.def: 以物易物、交易、以物易物、交換
barter.ex: They bartered their grain for salt.

base.def: 基礎、基地、卑劣的、低下的、以...作基礎、把...建立在
base.ex: The report is based on scientific research.

basement.ex: The laundry room is in the basement.

bashful.ex: She gave him a bashful smile.

basic.def: 基本的、基礎的、鹼性的、基本原理、基本設施、實例地、基於地
basic.ex: Food and shelter are basic needs.

basically.ex: Basically, I agree with your proposal.

basin.ex: The Amazon is the world's largest river basin.

basis.col: on a regular basis, on the basis of
basis.ex: The decision was made on the basis of political considerations.

basket.def: 籃子、一籃之量、籃框、把...裝入籃內
basket.ex: She carried the fruits in a small basket.

bass.def: 男低音、低音部、低音提琴、鱸魚、低音的、低沉的
bass.ex: He plays the bass guitar in the band.

bastard.def: 私生子、混蛋、雜交種、私生的、偽造的、平庸的、令人遺憾地、糟糕地

bat/batch.def: 蝙蝠、球棒、一批、一組、用球棒打、眨眼、成批生產、分批處理
bat/batch.ex: He hit the ball with a wooden bat.

batter.def: 擊球手、麵糊、連擊、猛擊、打壞、磨損

battle.def: 戰鬥、鬥爭、戰役、鬥爭、戰鬥、奮鬥
battle.ex: The two armies were preparing for battle.

battlefield.ex: Many soldiers died on the battlefield.
